style_anchors: 1890s botanical specimen plate by Pierre-Joseph Redouté
hand-drawn ink line engraving with minimal watercolor wash
cream-colored aged paper, muted ochre / sepia / olive green tones
no text, no labels, no typography, no captions, no annotations

anatomy.label: Anatomy — Xiao Long Bao cross-section
anatomy.subject_clause: A single Xiao Long Bao sliced open along its vertical axis to reveal the internal anatomy: the pleated wheat-flour skin in cross-section showing dough thickness, the steaming pork-and-aspic filling at the core, and clear amber soup pooling at the bottom of the cavity; the dumpling rests on a small bamboo steamer disk visible in side profile.
anatomy.composition_clause: Single specimen centered on the page in three-quarter cutaway view, anatomical-illustration framing, plenty of negative space around the subject.

component.label: Component — Xiao Long Bao dipping condiments set
component.subject_clause: A laid-out specimen plate of the three condiment components served alongside Xiao Long Bao: a small ceramic dish of julienned ginger threads, a small dish of dark Zhenjiang black vinegar with a porcelain spoon, and an empty round bamboo steamer disk; the three objects arranged in a triangular composition like separate botanical specimens on the same plate.
component.composition_clause: Three separate components arranged in a balanced triangular layout on the page, each rendered as if it were an individual specimen, no overlapping; ample negative space between items.

ingredient.label: Ingredient — Xiao Long Bao raw ingredients
ingredient.subject_clause: A laid-out specimen plate of the three raw ingredients that make Xiao Long Bao: a small mound of fine white wheat flour, a portion of fresh ground pork showing its texture, and a small bundle of scallion stalks and ginger root with skin partially peeled; the three items arranged side by side like botanical specimens on the same plate.
ingredient.composition_clause: Three raw ingredients laid out in a horizontal specimen row on the page, each rendered as its own discrete object, no overlapping; ample negative space between items.
